rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when describing interface behavior, and in implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers and named constants to define bounds and relations in AGREE contracts.
Structure AGREE equations and constants within component type annexes for interface-level assumptions and guarantees.
Encode boolean logic and range checks using valid AGREE expression syntax with visible feature names.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax includes `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` declarations within `annex agree` blocks.
Component type contracts should not reference subcomponent ports directly unless represented through variables or outputs.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level `assign` clauses in component type annexes; use implementation scopes instead.
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties as AGREE variables unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing parameters not directly tied to visible model features
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
